Technology

LightningWire™ TPS

The LightningWire™ Transseptal Puncture System (TPS), which combines a novel electrosurgical guidewire with a dedicated Activation Cable to simplify procedures using preferred transseptal introducers and existing electrosurgical generators, is now 510(k)‑cleared by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to create an atrial septal defect in the heart, whereby various cardiovascular catheters are introduced.

The LightningWire TPS is designed to maximize electrosurgical performance, mechanical performance, compatibility, efficiency, safety, and value. Minimally invasive catheter ablation, left atrial appendage occlusion, and transcatheter mitral valve procedures are all streamlined.

Technology

Two components, one controlled puncture.

LightningWire separates the transseptal step into a guidewire built for handling and an activation cable built for control — so the puncture happens on cue, not by feel alone.

LightningWire Guidewire

  • Proprietary insulation design optimizes electrosurgical performance and mechanical properties along the length of the wire
  • Robust distal insulation allows transseptal puncture at low power settings, starting at 15 W1
  • 0.032‑inch labeled diameter facilitates broad compatibility with various transseptal devices, including Agilis™ and Swartz™ SL1™
  • More than 2× stiffer than the market‑leading 0.035‑inch guidewire to support transseptal delivery of bulky ablation and structural heart devices2

Activation Cable

  • Connects to separately cleared electrosurgical generators via industry‑standard monopolar accessory ports
  • Does not require a dedicated electrosurgical generator
  • Designed to activate only desirable "cut" RF waveforms; cannot activate undesirable "coag" waveforms, which might lead to unwanted thrombus generation3
  • Puts control of radiofrequency energy in the operator’s hands on the sterile field, including audible and haptic feedback
In Detail

What's in the system.

Images above may show compatible third‑party components for demonstration only. No affiliation or endorsement is implied.

Applications

Built for the transseptal step in three procedures.

Catheter Ablation

Minimally invasive access for left‑heart ablation procedures.

Left Atrial Appendage Occlusion

Reliable septal crossing ahead of device delivery.

Transcatheter Mitral Valve Procedures

Consistent access for transcatheter mitral repair and replacement.

Why LightningWire

Efficiency, safety, and value in one system.

Efficiency

Fewer exchanges, faster workflow

Guidewire‑based transseptal puncture minimizes catheter exchanges. The Activation Cable immediately disconnects after transseptal puncture to enable therapy device delivery, and visible markers facilitate fluoroscopy‑free workflows.

Safety

Designed to limit injury risk

Compared to crossing at higher power settings, such as 50 W, lower power settings have been associated with reduced thermal injury to inter‑atrial septal tissue.4 Pigtail tip shape minimizes risk of cardiovascular injury after transseptal puncture.5

Value

Works with equipment already in the lab

Since existing electrosurgical generators may be used, capital expense may be eliminated. Disposable cost is competitive with alternatives.
